name: Monitor Redis with INFO, SLOWLOG, and Latency
description: Track Redis health, performance bottlenecks, and slow commands using built-in diagnostic tools for production observability.

steps.title: Get a full server overview
steps.description: Retrieve comprehensive server stats including memory, clients, and keyspace.
steps.tip: Use 'redis-cli INFO memory' or 'redis-cli INFO stats' to filter to a specific section.

steps.title: Check memory usage breakdown
steps.description: Inspect detailed memory allocation and fragmentation ratio.

steps.title: Review slow commands
steps.description: List the most recent commands that exceeded the slow log threshold.
steps.tip: Default threshold is 10ms. Lower it with 'CONFIG SET slowlog-log-slower-than 5000' (microseconds).

steps.title: Enable latency monitoring
steps.description: Activate the latency monitor to track spikes over time.

steps.title: View latency history
steps.description: Display recorded latency events and their timestamps.

steps.title: Monitor commands in real time
steps.description: Stream all commands being processed by Redis for live debugging.
steps.warning: MONITOR adds overhead and outputs every command. Use briefly for debugging and never in production for extended periods.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Redis Monitoring Dashboard
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Server Overview ==="
redis-cli INFO server | grep -E "redis_version|uptime_in_days|connected_clients"

echo ""
echo "=== Memory ==="
redis-cli INFO memory | grep -E "used_memory_human|used_memory_peak_human|mem_fragmentation_ratio"

echo ""
echo "=== Stats ==="
redis-cli INFO stats | grep -E "total_commands_processed|keyspace_hits|keyspace_misses|expired_keys|evicted_keys"

HITS=$(redis-cli INFO stats | grep keyspace_hits | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d '\r')
MISSES=$(redis-cli INFO stats | grep keyspace_misses | cut -d: -f2 | tr -d '\r')
if [ "$((HITS + MISSES))" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Cache hit rate: $(echo "scale=2; $HITS * 100 / ($HITS + $MISSES)" | bc)%"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Slow Log (last 5) ==="
redis-cli SLOWLOG GET 5

echo ""
echo "=== Latency ==="
redis-cli LATENCY LATEST

echo ""
echo "=== Connected Clients ==="
redis-cli CLIENT LIST | wc -l
echo "total connections"

echo ""
echo "=== Keyspace ==="
redis-cli INFO keyspace

faq.question: What is a healthy memory fragmentation ratio?
faq.answer: A ratio between 1.0 and 1.5 is normal. Above 1.5 means significant fragmentation and wasted memory. Below 1.0 means Redis is using swap, which severely impacts performance.

faq.question: How do I reduce the number of slow log entries?
faq.answer: Identify patterns in SLOWLOG GET output. Common fixes include adding indexes, avoiding O(N) commands like KEYS or SMEMBERS on large sets, and using SCAN instead of KEYS.

faq.question: Can I export Redis metrics to Prometheus?
faq.answer: Yes, use the redis_exporter project (oliver006/redis_exporter). It scrapes INFO and exposes metrics at a /metrics endpoint that Prometheus can scrape.
